summary: The user is planning a trip to Sequoia National Park and seeks recommendations for hiking trails, specifically the Congress Trail. They inquire about trail conditions, parking availability, and photography tips to enhance their experience while enjoying a picnic lunch amidst the giant sequoias.
facts: The user has experience with long drives and hiking in national parks, including a two-night stay at the Yosemite Valley Lodge during a road trip to Yosemite National Park. The user is interested in hiking the Congress Trail at Sequoia National Park. The Congress Trail is a well-maintained trail with a mix of paved and dirt surfaces, with restrooms and water stations available at specific locations. The user plans to visit the General Sherman Tree and Congress Trail early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid crowds. The user intends to bring a picnic lunch to enjoy at the trailhead or along the Congress Trail. The General Sherman Tree parking area can fill up quickly, especially during peak season, and alternative parking options include the Wolverton Creek and Giant Forest Museum parking areas. The user plans to bring a camera to capture views of the giant sequoias and seeks photography tips for the best shots.
